task_completion.description: The browser is open and navigated to the DuckDuckGo Onion service.
The browser's Developer Tools are open with the Network tab active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Developer Tools panel is visible at the bottom or side of the browser, and the 'Network' tab is selected.

task_completion.description: The required search query is entered into DuckDuckGo.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The search box or the search results page clearly displays the query 'tor_network_perf_audit_9921'.

task_completion.description: Network traffic for the search is recorded in the Network tab.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Network tab is populated with network requests (e.g., document, XHR, images) related to loading the DuckDuckGo search results.

task_completion.description: The captured network traffic is exported as a HAR file to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog shows the file being saved as 'onion_performance_baseline.har' in the '/home/ga/Documents' folder, or a file manager/terminal confirms the file exists at this exact path.

integrity.description: The agent used a real web browser to access the Onion site.
integrity.visual_evidence: A genuine browser interface (like Tor Browser or Firefox) is visible, not a terminal using curl or a text editor.

integrity.description: The network capture appears to be a genuine recording of web traffic.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Network tab shows realistic request timings, statuses, and sizes, rather than being empty or manually mocked.

integrity.description: The HAR file was exported using the browser's built-in functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen interacting with the 'Save all as HAR' context menu or export button in the Developer Tools, or the save dialog is triggered from the browser.
